questions.text: The slope of a line parallel to x-axis is:
questions.explanation: A line parallel to x-axis has slope 0 because there is no change in y-coordinate.

questions.text: Two lines are perpendicular if the product of their slopes is -1.
questions.explanation: If m1 and m2 are slopes of two perpendicular lines, then m1 × m2 = -1

questions.text: The equation of line with slope m and y-intercept c is ________.
questions.explanation: Slope-intercept form of line: y = mx + c, where m is slope and c is y-intercept.

questions.text: Find the distance between points (1, 2) and (4, 6).
